title: Natural Spoken English
topics: fillers · vague language · ellipsis · informal contractions · discourse
description: Přirozená mluvená angličtina — výplňková slova, neformální zkratky a plynulost.
notes: ⚠️  Fillers are fine in SPEECH but avoid in formal writing: 'I mean', 'you know', 'like'
✓ Ellipsis in speech: 'Coming?' (= Are you coming?) / 'Sounds good.' / 'Done.'
✓ British: 'Fancy a coffee?' = 'Do you want a coffee?'
⚠️  'gonna/wanna/gotta' = acceptable in fast informal speech, NOT in writing

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Hey, you wanna grab lunch or something?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Yeah, hang on — I'm just finishing this email.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: No worries. I reckon the café round the corner is decent.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Fair enough. I mean, I'm kind of craving a sandwich anyway.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Right, let's go. You coming, Tom?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Cheers, yeah. Be there in a sec.

examples.en: It was sort of interesting, you know?
I reckon we should leave by 7.
examples.cz: Myslím, že bychom měli odejít v 7.

examples.en: She's gonna be late again, isn't she?
Fancy going for a walk later?

quiz.sentence: I ___ we should take the train. (informal 'think')
That's ___ good news, I suppose. (sort of)

tasks: 1. Listen to a British podcast for 10 minutes — write down 5 fillers or informal expressions you hear
2. Write a spoken-style dialogue (WhatsApp conversation) using natural spoken English
3. Rewrite this formal text as informal speech: 'I was wondering whether you would be available to meet on Friday.'
